fields.text: Who are u buying a gift for?
fields.description: Research shows that the closer we are to someone, the more likely we are to choose gifts that bring joy rather than just practicality. Let's start by understanding your relationship.
No psychology here - just practical math. We'll show you gifts that fit your price range, from thoughtful little things to premium surprises.
Gifts for kids change dramatically with age — a toy for a 2-year-old is very different from one for a 10-year-old. Help us get it right.
Boys and girls often have different preferences — even at a young age. This helps us narrow down the perfect gift.
Did you know? Gifts given "just because" are often appreciated even more than birthday presents. But every occasion has its own vibe.
This is where the magic happens. Psychologists say the best gifts connect with a person's passions. Choose the ones that fit best - the more, the better!

fields.text: What matters most in a gift?
fields.description: According to research, people value gifts differently - some want practicality, others want surprise. What would they appreciate more?
Color psychology is real - favorite colors say a lot about personality. Plus, it helps us find gifts that match their vibe.
A minimalist and a maximalist see the world differently — and they like different gifts. Let's find their aesthetic.

fields.text: How would you describe their personality?
fields.description: The final piece of the puzzle. Personality influences everything - from color choices to gift preferences. This helps us fine-tune the recommendations.
